instruction:
Consider the set $\{1,2,\dots,a\}$ and all its nonempty subsets, which we'll denote as $F_1, F_2, \dots, F_{2^a-1}$ in any arbitrary order. Now, construct a matrix $R$ of size $(2^a-1) \times (2^a-1)$ where each element $m_{ij}$ is defined as follows: \[ m_{ij} = \begin{cases} 1 & \text{if $F_i$ and $F_j$ have at least one element in common,} \\ 0 & \text{if $F_i$ and $F_j$ are disjoint.} \end{cases} \]Your task is to evaluate the determinant of $R$. In case multiple determinant values are possible, compute the sum of all distinct determinant values.

Options:
A. -1
B. -2
C. 3
D. 4
E. 1
F. 5
G. -3
H. 0
I. -4
J. 2
